segments.text: Thank you for watching!
In this verse, the Lord is addressed as Keshava because he killed the demon Keshī in the beginning of creation.
Keshava is another name of Krishna.
Krishna is the origin of all incarnations and is confirmed in the Brahma-samhita that Govinda, the Supreme Personality of Godhead,
the cause of all causes, exists simultaneously in a different incarnation and expansions.
The demon's attempt to measure the Supreme Personality of Godhead is significant.
The demon wanted to embrace him with his arms, thinking that with his limited arms he could capture the Absolute by material power.
He did not know that God is greatest of the great and the smallest of the small.
No one can capture the Supreme Lord or bring him under his control.
But the demoniac persons always attempt to measure the length and the breadth of the Supreme Lord.
By his inconsiderate potency, the Lord can become the universal form, as explained in the Bhagavad Gita,
and at the same time he can remain within the box of his devotees as his worshipable Deity.
There are many devotees who keep a statue of the Lord in a small box and carry it with them everywhere.
Every morning they worship the Lord in the box.
The Supreme Lord, Keshava, or the Personality of Godhead, Krishna, is not bound by any measurement of our calculation.
He can remain with his devotee in any suitable form, yet he is unapproachable by any amount of demoniac activities.
So this chapter named, The Killing of Demon Hiranaksha,
and here we are seeing how in the fight between the demon and the Lord, finally the demon is getting defeated.
When the demon saw his magic forces dispelled all his powers, all his whatever ammunition he had,
he tried everything to defeat the Lord,
finally, when he came in the presence of the Supreme Lord,
personality of Godhead, Keshava, full of rage, as a last attempt, he wanted to embrace him,
but with a different intention. Out of rage, he wanted to embrace him with his arm in order
to crush him, so that he can embrace the Lord and embrace so tightly that the Lord is crushed
and killed. But to his amazement, when he tried doing that, when he embraced the Lord
tightly in order to crush him, to his great amazement, he found the Lord standing outside
the circle of his arms.
So, Prabhupada, in the
Bible, explains that the Lord has inconceivable potencies. He is smaller than the smallest,
bigger than the biggest, finer than the finest. When the demon wanted to embrace him with
his arm, thinking that with his limited arms he could capture the absolute by his material
prowess, powers, he did not know that God is greater than the greatest and smaller than
the smallest of all the small.
So as we become devotees, we become aware of the magnanimity of the Lord, how the Lord
is so glorious, his wonderful qualities. And when devotees hear that, they rejoice.
Their appreciation of the Lord.
devotion to the Lord, love for the Lord increases. Because to be a devotee, one fundamental requisite
is anusuyave. Although as conditioned souls, still we may have an enjoying tendency, still
we may have different anarthas, but a very fundamental step which all of us have taken
is to agree that we are subordinate to the Lord. We want to serve the Lord. Our conditioning
might force us to act in different ways. We may act otherwise. Our actions may exhibit
that we still have lording tendency, we still have enjoying tendency, but fundamentally
yes we accept Krishna's to Bhagwan Swayam. He is the enjoyer, He is the proprietor, He
is the Lord, He is the master.
He is the supreme controller. Whereas demons don't accept that. They want to exert their
own supremacy, and that's what here Hrnaksha is exhibiting, even to the extent of killing
the Lord. They don't realize that one cannot capture
the Absolute.
Supreme Lord by their material prowess. Even Ravana out of his tapasya wanted to achieve
so much of power, Hiranyakashipu. These are all demoniac tendencies. With little common
sense we can understand that Lord is our maintainer. Everything we have actually belongs to the
Lord. Even we have got births from our parents, actually they were instrumental in giving
us birth. They are not the ultimate cause. The entire material energy is moving under
the supervision of the Supreme Lord. Mayadhyakshena prakrati suyate saa chara charam. So devotee
when he, his vision becomes, when his vision becomes, when his vision becomes, when his
vision becomes cleared, when he becomes enlightened, Divya Gyan, Hrithya Prakashito, then he is
able to recognize, realize, Swayam eva, what is that? Swayam evahi, supoorati adaha. Pratyaksha
avagmam, it becomes pratyaksha, it becomes very evident that he is the maintainer. He
is the one who is giving us everything. These days people are talking about power failures,
summers are approaching, there is not enough water in the reservoirs. Although man can
make different arrangements, ultimately the final arrangement is done by the Supreme Lord.
If it does not rain, you may have power plants but it won't run. So devotees can see that
whereas demoniac make plans that how minus the Lord we can control things.
Even to the extent of keeping God out of picture.
Vishwanath Chakravarti Thakur in one of his books mentioned about four kinds of good and
bad people. It's very nice where he analyzes that there are four kinds of good people.
One, although broad category is a good man or a bad man, he is a devotee or he is a demon,
but he further classifies a devotee and a demoniac person. The first category is mahat.
Vishwanath mahat sevaya. Mahat, he is a great person. And what is the quality of a mahat?
A Mahat, these all first four categories are of, you know, people who have started becoming
devotees, started developing good qualities. Mahat, a great person has ability to see some
good in everyone. We all know in this world, we all have a good sense. In this world, we
We encounter different kinds of people, some very bad people, some very wicked people, cruel people and sometimes very charitably disposed, very pious, very good, very kind, noble souls.
All kinds of people are there in this world and sometimes it is very bewildering.
A Mahat has the ability to see some good in everyone.
They also perceive fault in others.
Fault is also there.
But they consider that these faults can eventually be transformed by certain methods into good qualities.
It should be noted that Mahat will never reject or condemn a faulty person, but thinks only how that person can be benefited, can be transformed.
In other words, he sees mainly the good qualities, although he is able to see the faults, but he has that confidence.
That by following certain process, some principles, he can be transformed.
And those faults can be rectified and transformed into good qualities.

Such a person who has this kind of a mentality, he focuses on the good, he sees the fault, but he has the confidence that that can be rectified and transformed into a nice person, this is a quality of a Mahat.
Higher than Mahat is Mahattara.
A greater person.
First is great person, Mahat.
Greater person, Mahattara.
Mahattara also sees good and bad qualities in others, but focuses on and praises the good quality.
He gives an example just like a merchant.
He is a materialistic person.
He is very busy transacting, making money.
A typical businessman wants to increase his wealth.
Now, a Mahattara sees that a businessman very enthusiastically doing business.
Although he may see him as a sense gratifier, ultimately he is materialistic.
He wants to make more profit for himself and enjoy the profit.
But he notices that he is treating his customers so nicely.
And because he is so gentle in his dealings, he is worthy of deliverance.
So, in other words, he praises the good quality.
Although he sees the fault, he magnifies the good qualities.
Then, Vishnu Chakravidh Thakur, even higher than Mahattara is Mahattama, especially great person.
Even greater than that person.
A Mahattama.
A Mahattama sees whatever good qualities others have, he magnifies them, not seeing the even smallest of faults.
He refuses to focus on the faults.
He sees good qualities and he magnifies them.
If he sees 80% good, 20% fault, he ignores the 20% and the 80%, he magnifies it to 100%.
In other words, he focuses only on the good.
So, Vishnu Chakravidh describes this attitude, this person has stolen my cloth.
Suppose a devotee, we have this kind of issues in ashram.
Suppose somebody has stolen the cloth which was, let's say, kept for drying.
Now, one thing is you can curse the other person.
He has stolen, how dare he do that, what does he think of himself, xyz.
He has done it.

doing philanthropic activities, working for somebody's else benefit, a sadhu will think
this is just an outward show. Actually, he has some ulterior motive, he has some agenda.
Suppose somebody is helping this thing, so finally somebody will even ask him, what do

seeing that the person is helping, he is seeing the ulterior motive, focuses on that and says
that actually this is all a show.
A sadhu tara, very wicked person. The next category is a sadhu tara is even more improper.
He or she only sees fault in others and overlooks other good qualities. As I mentioned here,
80-20, 80% is good, 20% is false. He ignores 80% good, focuses on 20% fault and sees that
only as a good thing. He or she only sees fault in others and overlooks other good qualities.
only as the qualities available in a person. He gives an example, let's say sanyasi is
eating a feast, let's say. Without seeing that he is a sanyasi, he is a devotee, he
has surrendered his life, his devotion, his love for the Lord, he sees that he is lusty,
he doesn't have control on his tongue and very soon he will fall down.
He is a sanyasi, he is honouring prasadam, maybe it's a feast, it's some festival, so
rather than focusing on the good qualities, Prabhupada was also complained by some devotees
or devotees are very pushy in the airports, they force people sometimes. So somebody wrote
a letter to Prabhupada, Prabhupada says, please forgive them, at least recognise the fact
that they have surrendered their lives to the Lord.
They are working for the Lord, they are serving the Lord. This is very, very important even
for us in our daily lives because it all depends on what we focus on. Typically that example
is given, glass half empty or half filled. Some people can focus on that it is half filled,
some people can focus on it is half empty.
The next category is asadutama.
Asadutama is a very, very special wicked person. An asadutama takes small faults and
magnifies them, seeing no good qualities at all.
Vishwanachakravarti Thakur gives an example, asadutama will criticise and condemn a renunciant
as a bogus rascal for the fault of collecting donations.
Asadutama will conclude, he has come to fleece money, he has come to somehow by hook or crook
take money from us. Rather than seeing that he is a sadhu, they are begging alms.
Prabhupada says in Vedic times, it is expected that the grahastha's maintain the brahmanas
because the brahmanas, they are busy with pathan pathan, yajan yajan. They are busy
So rather than seeing that he has come, now mind you, unfortunately in present time,
many people have taken advantage of the dress, but otherwise they are genuine sadhus who are dependent on the society.
So rather than seeing that, he says he is a wicked rascal.

if the devotion is not there, bhava is not there, love is not there, you may even have

So it is very unfortunate in present times we see this demoniac tendencies. Just few

are now atheists. They don't believe in God. And the number has increased when compared
to 5 years back or 10 years back. We are living in a very, very materialistic world where
people.
So again, Krishna has it's own Leela as time progresses because of intense desire to Lord
over material nature. Krishna also sanctions to some extent. And people with all this material
advancement they perceive that yes, now we are in control of things. To some extent they
are able to see that you know a lot of things, the data they can analyze. They can control
the outcomes. They can plan and execute the plans.
make the required profits. They are trying to make some formula. If you follow the formula,
this will be the desired results. Nothing but lording tendency. Five factors of action,
Krishna says, the ultimate, the chief is the super soul. That is completely out of the

aggrandize, we may become wealthy, we may accumulate some riches, but ultimately Krishna
says, finally, even if we don't surrender to the Lord, we don't recognize the Lord,
finally, mithyu harvas chaham, sarva haras chaham, finally everyone has to surrender
at the point.
of death. Even that, demoniac people are not able to recognize. That's why everyone
is so busy accumulating wealth, making properties, more and more, gratifying the senses. It's
very unfortunate. One time there was a wealthy man who was sitting in the balcony and he
saw there was an ant crossing the balcony from one side of the balcony to the other
side.
So he was very carefully noticing this ant and this ant, I am sure all of us have watched,
they also have intelligence. If you try to put a hand, obstruct the path, they will turn
left, turn right, somehow try to evade. Immediately they are able to sense there is an obstacle.
All that is so small, so insignificant, it has some analytical skills. It can sense there
is a danger.
You put a hand, it will turn left, you put hand there also it will turn the other side.
If you keep hand, it will start moving fast, it will turn back. It senses that now somebody
is obstructing path either ways.
So this person was carefully observing and he was really amazed that such a tiny creature
is having the ability to comprehend, analyze, explore, to some extent predict. He was able
to predict.
He was able to understand and predict danger. It would make twists and turns and keep moving.
And that ant was carrying some kind of a leaf.
Then comes in the floor a very big crack, must be an old house. There was a very big
crack. Imagine the crack was there and this ant is some 1 or 2 millimeter long. Imagine
the crack is 8 mm.
8 mm. No way this ant could have crossed the crack. So this person became little more curious
to understand what will the ant do now. Because if you obstruct, you can go left, you can
go right. Now there is an 8 mm crack and this ant is just 1 mm or 2 mm. What will the ant
do? He was curious to know. The ant came, sensed that there is a crack, there is an
obstruction. It was carrying a leaf, it placed the leaf on the crack, climbed on the leaf,
reached the other side of the crack, picked the leaf and started. The man was amazed that
so much intelligence they have. When the attack happens or something, there is a team of army
engineers who are trained to do this. They are trained to do this. They are trained to
do this. They are trained to do this. They are trained to do this. They are trained to

that much intelligence this ant has? It was amazed. Finally the ant was carrying that
leaf and finally it was keenly observing, it reached the other end of the balcony and
he saw there was a small hole and it was quite obvious that that is the place where the ant
stays.

again fall into the clutches of death, let's turnam, quickly, there's a sense of eagerness,
let's accumulate more and more of spiritual assets. Because these are the assets which
we will carry with us, which the soul, atma will carry with us. Otherwise, no matter how
big leaf, how much property we have on our back, it's just a burden, it will be left
behind. So, here the demon is exhibiting that kind of a quality. He wants to even crush
the Lord, be the Lord.
Not recognizing that one day he will be vanquished.
So, we will stop here, grantharaj, shimad bhagavatam ki, ishla prabhupada ki.
